sub_mechanisms: M_1: compute discrete tangent-normal-binormal at each token via consecutive embedding differences
M_2: estimate per-token κ (local curvature magnitude)
M_3: estimate per-token τ (local torsion magnitude)
M_4: gate attention bandwidth proportional to κ (high κ = wider context)
stripped_llm_application: At each token position, compute three orthonormal direction vectors from consecutive hidden states (analogous to local curve frame); estimate a local curvature magnitude and a local torsion magnitude; gate the attention bandwidth by the curvature magnitude — high curvature widens the context window for that token.
novelty_claim_stripped: 'Gating Enables Curvature' (2604.14702) studies gating's effect on Fisher-Rao curvature of the statistical manifold; this candidate computes EUCLIDEAN local curve curvature κ on the hidden-state trajectory and uses it as a GATING SIGNAL not as a property to be analyzed.
